PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Dec-01-2009 Continued To Mar-02-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for and with Barbara DeVries (Trustee). Ernest Der for the Conservatee Roselia Yere. Erlinda Ipac, Rogelio Ipac, Elizabeth Ipac, Gloria Scherpf, Elizabeth Ipac and Julia Formoso (children of the Conservatee) also present in Propria Persona.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the Conservatee being returned to her home versus remaining in the nursing home, the house being sold or rented out, visitation, Rogelio Ipac being appointed Conservator or Barbara DeVries remaining as the Conservator, and the issue of Elizabeth finding housing and funds being advanced to be able to do so. The Court stated that the Conservatee was in the best place to receive the care she needed, that there was still some degree of dispute between the Family members, that it was time for Elizabeth to find housing and order that the house be sold. No listing agreement or contract is to be signed for 60 days. An ex parte application for the amount of the loan needed for Elizabeth to move out will be filed prior to the next hearing date if a plan is in place. The matter was continued to March 2, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the sale of the house and placement for Elizabeth and her Son.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
